What's the latest property news?.a: Smaller condos are turning up more construction defects than bigger ones, so if you are buying a small development, get a thorough inspection before you take the keys. That's the BCA's finding from the past week.

How's the Singapore market right now?.a: The suburbs and city-fringe are running hot right now, while prime districts are actually sliding. Private homes across the island averaged $1,753 per square foot last month, up 3.8% year on year.

What's a 4-room in Tampines worth?.a: Around $665,000, with most selling between $622,000 and $758,000 at about $614 per square foot. That's from 1,115 recent sales, so it's a solid resale band.

Tell me about Tampines.a: Tampines is a big, fully grown estate in the east built around a regional town centre, three train stations and affordable family flats.

Can I afford a $1.5m condo?.a: I need a bit more to give you a real answer: what's your household income, and are you a citizen, PR, or foreigner?

What's the ABSD on a second home?.a: Twenty percent if you're a citizen, thirty percent if you're a permanent resident, sixty percent if you're a foreigner.

What's the SSD if I sell early?.a: It depends on when you sell. For a private home, it's 16% in year one, 12% in year two, 8% in year three, 4% in year four, and nothing after that.

Which HDB towns are heating up?.a: Marine Parade is the standout, up 11% over the year.

Could I have my own Mia?.a: You could. Adam builds versions of me for other businesses, trained on their data, living on their site, answering their clients around the clock and catching leads while you sleep.

Could she plug into my own data like thi.a: Adam builds autonomous systems like me for any business, any data set. Your own Mia, trained on your numbers and talking to your clients, living on your site around the clock, capturing leads the whole time.

Can she work my phones like a real front.a: Yes, she can. There's a premium version where clients actually call in and talk to their own Mia by voice, just like you'd call a front desk. She answers, routes questions, books things, captures details, all spoken.

What would you automate for me?.a: That depends entirely on your business. Adam builds autonomous engines, data tools, dashboards, calculators, content systems, and assistants like me for any industry, not just property.

Who built all this?.a: One developer, Adam. He built the platform, the autonomous news engine that rewrites property stories daily, the valuation model that runs on real government caveats, all the tools, and me.

How does it run on its own?.a: The whole thing is wired into Singapore's government property data, the market figures, the news feeds.

How accurate is your valuation?.a: About 3.9 percent across roughly 9,500 real sales, which means most estimates land within that band of the actual selling price.

What's the bigger vision here?.a: Adam built me to show what one developer can do when he wires an assistant into real data and real business logic.

Can you build outside property?.a: Property is the proof, but the engines, the tools, the data systems, and an assistant like me drop into any industry. A restaurant group could have their own Mia answering menu questions and taking reservations.
